L1.area: Remote file handling and product data synchronization logic

L2.description: A server-side request forgery vulnerability exists where a user-supplied URL intended for a product image is fetched by the server without validating the target host against an allowlist. Additionally, the image validation check is susceptible to a time-of-check time-of-use race condition: a malicious server can return a valid response during the initial verification request but redirect to an arbitrary internal resource during the subsequent file retrieval, enabling full SSRF exploitation.

L3.area: Remote file validation utilities and product image synchronization in the network module
L3.description: A server-side request forgery vulnerability exists in the product image synchronization feature of the network module. A manufacturer account can supply an arbitrary URL as a product image, causing the server to fetch it without validating the target host against configured sync domains. The image validity check is also susceptible to a time-of-check time-of-use race condition: a malicious server returns a valid image on the initial HEAD request but issues a redirect to an internal resource on the subsequent GET request, enabling full SSRF exploitation.

ground_truth.reason: The remote file existence check and image validation use separate HTTP requests (HEAD then GET), creating a TOCTOU race condition. A malicious server can serve a valid image during the check but redirect to an internal resource during retrieval, combined with no host allowlist validation, enabling full SSRF.
